mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, directly generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden unemployment rate spike above 8.0% signals severe labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 8.0%, or signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand and wealth effects, driving sustained positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a win rate below 45% in signal periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and falling corporate revenues, producing negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, invalidating the supply constraint channel.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5% force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and generating negative 20-day forward returns as policy lag materializes.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate profit expectations, producing positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day SPX returns, with Rank IC < 0.01 over a 5-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer momentum, triggering rapid short-term equity sell-offs within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns, producing a win rate below 40% in signal periods over a 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals broad-based manufacturing contraction, reducing earnings forecasts and causing negative 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in >55% of observations when INDPRO crosses below its 12-month trend, or IC < 0.02.
